should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator that turns user goals into tool-using execution plans with feedback and recovery.
Our agent router works in demos but fails when APIs time out or tasks are ambiguous. Diagnose the architecture.
Map this planner into goals, outer environment constraints, inner mechanisms, interfaces, and evaluation loops.
Help me decompose a broad orchestration objective into subgoals, operators, stopping criteria, and verification points.
should_not_trigger: Summarize Herbert Simon's biography and major awards.
Write CSS for a pricing card component.
Explain the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning without designing a system.
edge_cases: I want a general design critique of our agent product. Trigger only if the response will analyze it as a purposeful artifact with goals, environment, inner mechanisms, interfaces, and adaptation evidence.

Compare two orchestration frameworks. Trigger if the comparison is about fit to environment, bounded-rationality tradeoffs, decomposition, and feedback loops; do not trigger for a generic feature checklist.

compliance_expectations.runtime_dependency: Must answer from SKILL.md content only; do not require external source files, webpages, source reports, original books, external source folder, or distilled source text.
compliance_expectations.activation_gate: Should trigger only when designing, diagnosing, or evaluating a purposeful artifact through goals, outer environment, inner mechanism, interfaces, and adaptation.
compliance_expectations.expected_behavior: Identify the artifact and preferred situation, then produce boundary, fit diagnosis, means-ends decomposition, satisficing tradeoffs, near-decomposable architecture, adaptation loop, and perturbation checks.
compliance_expectations.negative_behavior: Must not start with frameworks/tools before preferences and environment, claim global optimization under incomplete knowledge, or replace high-stakes domain review.
